name: SBAR Handover
description: Evaluates a handover to another healthcare professional using the Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation framework.

anchors.5-7: Understandable but occasional hesitations or grammar errors.
Mostly accurate, some colloquialisms instead of professional terms.
anchors.1-4: Misuses terms in ways that could cause clinical errors.

anchors.8-10: Clearly articulates Situation, Background, Assessment, and Recommendation.
anchors.5-7: Misses one component or jumbles the order.
anchors.1-4: Missing multiple components or highly disorganized.

anchors.8-10: Respectful, concise, and handles questions gracefully.
anchors.5-7: Mostly respectful but may get flustered.
anchors.1-4: Rude, defensive, or overly verbose without getting to the point.

checklist.item: Provided relevant Background (PMH, events leading up)
Gave a clear Assessment (suspected diagnosis or clinical status)
Made a specific Recommendation (admit, review, prescribe)
Confirmed the receiving clinician understood or accepted the plan
